news.headline: Growing up with sirens: UN child rights envoy on the toll of the Ukraine-Russia war
news.summary: Children in Ukraine have been profoundly impacted by years of war, sheltering in underground schools – or forced to study online – and living with the psychological strain of constant air raid sirens that could spell death for them and their families.

news.headline: World News in Brief: UN urges restraint as Gulf tensions rise, fear and uncertainty in Lebanon, hunger grows in the Sahel

news.headline: Kyrgyzstan wins seat on UN Security Council for first time ever
news.summary: A closely watched Security Council election delivered a mix of continuity and change on Wednesday, as Austria, Portugal, Trinidad and Tobago, Zimbabwe – and first-time member Kyrgyzstan, secured seats around the iconic horseshoe table.

news.headline: Gaza’s public servants systematically targeted in Israeli strikes
news.summary: Months since Gaza’s nominal ceasefire began, Palestinians continue to be killed and maimed in drone and airstrikes, including the enclave’s police force which is crucial to peace and reconstruction efforts, the UN human rights office (OHCHR) said on Wednesday.

news.headline: Yemen: Hunger crisis deepens as funding cuts leave millions without support
news.summary: Nearly half of the population in Government-controlled areas of Yemen are facing high levels of acute food insecurity with the crisis set to deepen further if international aid cuts continue, according to the&nbsp;latest analysis by&nbsp;the leading UN-backed global food security platform.

news.headline: ‘We are catching up’ – WHO chief on DR Congo’s Ebola fight
news.summary: The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) said on Wednesday that the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is showing signs of progress – but significant challenges remain in testing, surveillance, vaccine development and building community trust.&nbsp;

news.headline: Regional health agency issues measles alert for World Cup
news.summary: As World Cup fever rises in the Americas, countries are urged to strengthen measles surveillance and vaccination amid ongoing outbreaks across the region.&nbsp;

news.summary: Women and girls in Lebanon are paying an increasingly devastating price as violence and displacement continue despite a ceasefire, the UN reproductive health agency (UNFPA) warned on Tuesday.

news.headline: UN mission reports rising tensions along the Blue Line in Lebanon
news.summary: Monday’s hastily convened meeting of the UN Security Council at the request of France in response to&nbsp;escalating violence in Lebanon between Israeli forces and Hezbollah militants has underlined deepening international concern as the conflict intensifies,&nbsp;despite ongoing US mediatio

news.headline: UN deplores another wave of Russian attacks across Ukraine
news.summary: Overnight attacks in three key cities in Ukraine have left several civilians dead, scores more injured, and homes, hospitals and shops destroyed or damaged, the UN Humanitarian Coordinator in the country said on Tuesday.&nbsp;

news.headline: Lebanon hospital strikes impact most vulnerable patients, warns WHO
news.summary: The UN health agency in Lebanon is verifying reports of strikes on a hospital in the southern city of Tyre on Monday, amid a concerning rise in attacks on healthcare in the country.

news.headline: UN urges world not to forget Rohingya refugees as aid cuts threaten vital support
news.summary: As the world approaches nine years since the mass displacement of Rohingya refugees from Myanmar into Bangladesh, the UN refugee agency (UNHCR) has appealed to the international community not to abandon the 1.2 million refugees living in the country, most of them in camps in Cox’s Bazar.

news.headline: Haiti: UN rights office welcomes new judicial units to tackle gang violence and impunity
news.summary: The UN&nbsp;human rights office, OHCHR,&nbsp;on Tuesday welcomed the opening of two specialised judicial units in Haiti, saying the move marked&nbsp;“a significant step toward tackling widespread impunity in the country.”&nbsp;

news.headline: UN stresses the need for de-escalation in Ukraine war as attacks rise
news.summary: The United Nations continues to warn against the dangerous escalation in the war in Ukraine, a senior official told the Security Council on Monday, underlining the need for restraint and dialogue.&nbsp;

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Lebanon as Israeli attacks casts pall over Iran-US negotiations
news.summary: The Security Council meets late on Monday at France’s request, as concern grows over escalating violence in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah amid warnings of Israeli strikes on Beirut’s southern suburbs, and confusion over the status of US-Iran peace talks linked to the faltering ceasefires. Fol

news.headline: From Libyan deserts to 3D-printed guns: The weapons that never go away
news.summary: Years after conflicts fade from the headlines, the weapons used to fight them often continue to circulate – crossing borders, fuelling crime and undermining an often-fragile peace. Now, ghost guns, 3D-printed firearms and increasingly sophisticated trafficking networks are creating new challenges fo

news.headline: Lebanon: Families flee Beirut as Israel threatens renewed strikes on Hezbollah
news.summary: As hostilities escalate in Lebanon despite a recent ceasefire extension, the United Nations continues to push for peace and support displaced civilians by providing food, protection and other assistance.

news.headline: DR Congo Ebola outbreak: Nurses discharged after full recovery
news.summary: Four nurses who fell ill with Ebola&nbsp;in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) have been discharged from hospital after recovering from the often-fatal illness that sparked an international health alert.&nbsp;

news.headline: Under fire, Kharkiv is already building for a peaceful tomorrow
news.summary: Every day in Kharkiv begins with uncertainty: air raid sirens interrupt sleep; missiles strike residential neighbourhoods, industrial sites, and roads. Anxious citizens rush into metro stations during bombardments and children study underground.

news.headline: Borrowed boots, borrowed joy: Football thrives in Gaza camps
news.summary: Matches organised by a former professional player are providing a brief respite from the harsh reality of life for the thousands living in overcrowded tents, schools or damaged buildings in the shattered Occupied Palestinian Territory of Gaza.

news.headline: ‘You are not alone’: WHO chief vows to stand with DR Congo through Ebola outbreak
news.summary: Community trust will be decisive in bringing the rapidly evolving Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo under control, WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said on Saturday, as health teams race to contain the emergency that has spread across multiple provinces and in

news.headline: World News in Brief: Drone strike in Romania condemned, Lebanon and South Sudan updates
news.summary: The UN Secretary-General has expressed alarm after a drone struck a residential building in the Romanian city of Galați overnight into Friday, reportedly injuring two people.

news.headline: Conflict-related sexual violence cases more than doubled in 2025, UN warns
news.summary: Nearly 10,000 cases of conflict-related sexual violence were recorded worldwide last year – more than double the previous year's figure – as rape, sexual slavery and abduction were deployed as weapons of war across Africa, the Middle East, Europe and the Caribbean.

news.headline: UN honours blue helmets and calls for renewed investment in peace
news.summary: Peacekeepers are serving in a dangerous era of mounting global tensions and tight resources. UN chief António Guterres is now calling for greater political backing and reliable financial support for ‘blue helmets’ who put themselves in harm’s way.

news.headline: Gaza: 26 killed over Eid holiday, UN rights investigators report
news.summary: At least 26 Palestinians were killed in the Gaza Strip since Tuesday – the eve of one of the most important holidays in Islam – the UN human rights office, OHCHR, reported on Friday.

news.headline: Gaza children trapped ‘in an endless cycle of suffering’: UNICEF
news.summary: The dire conditions in the Gaza Strip are trapping children “in an endless cycle of suffering” and their heartbroken parents can only look on, an official with the UN child rights agency UNICEF said on Friday in a fresh appeal for greater humanitarian access to support families in the war-ravaged en

news.headline: ‘A disease you get when you care for someone’: WHO on the Ebola frontline
news.summary: Two weeks into the latest deadly Ebola outbreak, the World Health Organization (WHO) estimates there are now 906 suspected cases of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), including 223 suspected deaths.

news.headline: UN warns Ukraine war risks spiralling ‘out of control’
news.summary: The United Nations on Thursday warned of a dangerous escalation in the war in Ukraine after a wave of large-scale Russian strikes and threats of further attacks, with Secretary-General António Guterres saying “the death spiral must stop.”

news.headline: World News in Brief: Lebanon violence continues, Israel ‘severs ties’ with UN chief, Internal displacement in Haiti, warning to France over prison overcrowding
news.summary: The UN has expressed deep concern over escalating hostilities in Lebanon following intensified Israeli airstrikes in southern Beirut and across southern parts of the country.

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Mounting attacks and Russian threats prompt emergency Ukraine meeting
news.summary: The UN&nbsp;Security Council is meeting in emergency session on Ukraine, with Secretary-General António Guterres telling ambassadors following what Kyiv describes as the most devastating attack on its capital to date, that “the time for peace is now.” In the early hours of 23-24 May, Russia laun

news.headline: WHO chief calls for ceasefire amid DR Congo Ebola outbreak
news.summary: The Director-General of the World Health Organization (WHO) headed to the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) on Thursday as the country continues to combat a deadly resurgence of Ebola in its volatile eastern region where instability is rife.&nbsp;

news.headline: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo collides with conflict and hunger, WHO warns
news.summary: The UN World Health Organization (WHO) on Wednesday warned that eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo faces a “catastrophic collision of disease and conflict” as a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak outpaces containment efforts in a region already battered by armed violence, mass displacement and acut

news.headline: Middle East update: Airstrikes and evacuation orders in Lebanon, aid restrictions in Gaza
news.summary: Intensified Israeli airstrikes overnight in Lebanon forced people to again flee their homes, while humanitarians in the Gaza Strip report continued restrictions in bringing aid into the enclave, the United Nations said on Tuesday.

news.headline: International airlines urged to stick to safety measures in wake of Ebola outbreak
news.summary: As a deadly Ebola strain continues to spread in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), with cases confirmed in neighbouring Uganda, the UN aviation agency is urging governments and flight operators to closely follow guidelines put in place following the COVID-19 pandemic.
